title: metroxylon inerme
intro: Metroxylon inerme, also known as the sago palm or sago tree, is a species of palm tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant's pith, or the central core of the stem, contains starch that can be extracted and used as a food source. The medicinal properties of Metroxylon inerme include its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, with its extracts exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Traditional herbal preparations of Metroxylon inerme involve boiling its pith to create a decoction that can be consumed to alleviate various health issues, including diarrhea, fever, and respiratory problems, and its health benefits include improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
